segments.text: Hi and welcome to The Winning Factor. I'm Alan Aitken and on this show each week
we take a look at some upcoming races in
Hong Kong, try to identify an angle to
the race that might lead us to finding the winner. Well it's Saturday racing
this weekend in Hong Kong and the first race
I'm going to take a look at is race
10 where our winning factor is the map. So this is class 3 1600 meters and
And Blazing Wukong and Amazing Partners are coming off last start wins.
Endured has been racing well in similar events without much luck.
Smart Avenue should improve with a better draw.
And Family Jewel and Hymnbook both look to have chances as well.
So not a one horse race by any means.
Quite a few chances.
But I'm looking most closely at a couple of horses I think will
cope with what looks quite a tricky event in terms of the map.
and that's Blazing Wukong and Amazing Partners. Now Amazing Partners first of
all well he's lightly raced he's only had six starts for three wins and this
was his latest when he tackled 1600 meters for the first time. This is race
601 he's in gate four pink colors and Amazing Partners jumps well he isn't a
quick horse naturally but Vincent Ho is able to get him into a position near the
lead through what is a slow first sectional before
the pace does pick up a little through
the middle stages but still is only steady.
A number of Saturday's rivals are here too.
In Jude inside Amazing Partners in red and grey,
Mr Dapper the leader and Himbuk in green
near the tail.
Now the field is bunched here with no serious
pace and becomes just a dash down the straight.
With In Jude on the inside having no luck
but Amazing Partners, well he's able to be
challenging at the right time with enough turn of
foot to reel in the leaders and he
grinds out the win.
Now despite his good winning record, three from six amazing partners, I don't really
think we've seen the best of him yet because
he's been in several of these kinds of races
where it's just a grinding sort of finish, difficult to get separation on the field.
So he's done well to put that record together.
And that could be a key thing in Saturday's race.
Now, Blazing Wukong, on the other hand,
he's a little bit more well-explored
with 11 starts so far for three wins.
And here's his latest offering.
This is race 643, gate 13 in black colours.
And Blazing Wukong jumps OK,
but jockey Andrea Azzini doesn't even think of looking
for a more forward position, going back to the rear. rear.
Now that prevents him covering extra ground early, but
we can see on the heat map
it's also a disadvantage with the slow early speed
here. The pace is a little better mid-race,
but never really gets above steady. Blazing Wukong then sprints well around the field,
deceptively fast around the field in fact, as they turn into the stretch, and he's the
one with the acceleration to go and pick up the leaders and put them away. way.
Now, just like Amazing Partners, not the most impressive
win to the eye, but what they have
in common is that each of them had to
produce acceleration at the right time, the right
place to go and win the race.
And as I said, I think that's going to
be important in the context of Saturday's race.
Now, the leaders, Mr Dapper and With All My Faith, have the outside gates.
So that is the hope of the side as far as getting a better speed.
speed, but it's quite possible they could get across quickly and then steady the brakes
and we might have a similar style of race to what we've been seeing.
There still looks a little speed in it, inside or
out, and that's not going to help the likes
of Family Jewel, Endured and others who have wider draws, while Hymnbook and Smart Avenue
have never really shown us much tactical speed yet, regardless of their draw. draw.
Now Blazing Wukong went all the way back last
time. We have seen him ridden like
that quite a bit, but he has also had a
lot of wider draws. This time he's got a better
draw so he might even be closer from barrier
five, but we do know that Amazing Partners
has the tactical speed to be where he needs to be again.
So the tip from me in Race 10 Amazing
Partners. His winning factor, the map. It does look
a very tricky map but it could be a stop-start kind of race. But what we've
seen from amazing partners even in those kinds of races is that he's able to put
himself in the right place, he's able to accelerate at the right time and that
might be the winning recipe for this event.
The other race I want to take a look at on Sunday is
the final event, Race 11 and our winning factor here, the draw.
So this is Class 2, an excellent Class 2 in fact, over 1200 metres.
And despite the talent in the race, I think
we can narrow this down to three really strong
chances.
A patch of stars who continues to race well,
and two horses we expect to see figuring in
some serious races in the future.
Jamie Richards' trained Bulb General, and the unbeaten three-year-old Hot Delight.
And I'm most interested in this race in those two lightly raced horses.
as I said Hot Delight unbeaten three from three and Bob General a year older
and he's three wins from five starts. Now firstly Hot Delight and his trainer
Francis Loy has made comparisons at the same stage with a horse that you might
be familiar with that he used to train Golden 60. That's a big call certainly
there's a lot of water to flow under the
bridge for that one but he can't be
faulted in what he's done so far.
This is race 540 he's in gate 7 in
yellow and
red colors and Hot Delight jumps well for Vincent Ho and is quickly up on the
speed in what you can see is a slow
first 400 metre section with Ho on the
odds on favorite here taking bad luck out of the equation. Now the pace does
lift a little as the race progresses then becomes a strong sprint home down
the stretch. The leader fights on well but ultimately Hot Delight is too good
and comes clear to win with some conviction. Now perhaps to the eye not
deserving the kind of boom that we're talking about on this horse but the way
the race was run there meant that to win
it the way he did Hot Delight had to
break 44 seconds for his last 800 meters and that's the kind of sustained speed
that is going to make this young horse a star
of the future as he develops and grows stronger.
As to Bulb General, well as I mentioned he's a
year older and really he was this time last year
where Hot Delight is now.
He was an up-and-comer with a great turn of
foot as a three-year-old.
He took that into his early starts as a four-year-old but then his career was interrupted
by injury.
He's only had the one run back in March and
let's have a look at how he
handled that. This is race 562, gate 7 here in orange and white colours.
Bulb General jumps well for Zach Pertin but this race is a contrast with the one
that we just saw as the early speed here is pretty good. Zach lets Bulb General
find his feet as is usual and he ends up towards the rear with another of
Saturday's rivals Patch of Stars in yellow, white and blue a few lengths
ahead of him on the rail. Now the pace increases again mid-race so it's really
run to suit horses off the pace and when they straighten up Patch of Stars comes
to pounce he comes up short behind Young Champion with Bulb General working home
steadily into third. Now Bulb General will meet the first two that beat him
home there on Saturday but they were hard race fit horses and he was coming
off quite a lengthy break so I do think
he's going to tighten up and be a better
version of himself on Saturday but I think when we go to the draw that's
where things are really getting interesting in this race. There's good
speed out wide here from Victor the winner Crimson Flash and Ladies Choice
so the tempo looks sound enough. Bob General is drawn lower this time while
while he's still not going to be on the front
end of the race. His jockey this time, Joe
Morera, should be able to use that draw to
get a nice trip through the race. Harry Bentley
takes over on Hot Delight with the light weight, but
his map looks filled with a lot of decisions
to make, and tough ones they are.
We saw Hot Delight up on the speed his last
two starts in slow tempos, but with a better pace and
a wide draw here, he would have trouble getting
getting to a similar spot without covering ground and working and might even have to
drag all the way back in the field so that he doesn't have a difficult run.
So the tip from me in race 11, Bulb General.
His winning factor, the draw.
Now had they drawn the other way around I'm
sure that I would have been tipping Hot Delight
but his job looks full of difficulties.
He's good enough to overcome them.
I certainly don't want to say it's lights out
for his chances, but it brings him back
to the older horse who has drawn better this
time, Bulb General, and should be fitter than
he was for that first up run.
Well that's it from the Winning Factor this week.
Good luck on Saturday, we'll see you next time.
